title.en: Clearing the air: "I think I misunderstood."

description.en: Misunderstandings happen. Learn how to admit a mistake gracefully using the phrase "오해했던 것 같아요" to clear the air without making it awkward.
description.ko: 상대방의 행동을 잘못 오해해서 마음이 불편할 때! 먼저 다가가 오해를 인정하고 관계를 회복하는 정중한 리액션을 배워봅니다.

tags: Situation. Social. Communication. Misunderstanding. Apology. Relationship. Season 2

content.speaker: John
content.text: Mina, I felt a bit ignored because my coworker didn't reply to my message all day. I thought he was mad at me.

content.speaker: John
content.text: But it turns out he just lost his phone! Now I feel terrible for being cold to him. How can I clear this up without making it a big deal?

content.speaker: Mina
content.text: 정말 민망한 상황이네요! 하지만 먼저 다가가서 솔직하게 말하면 상대방도 충분히 이해해 줄 거예요.
content.translation: Ah, that's a really embarrassing situation! But if you approach them first and speak honestly, they will surely understand.

content.speaker: Mina
content.text: 이럴 땐..., "제가 오해했던 것 같아요" 라는 표현을 써보세요.
content.translation: In these cases, try using the expression "I think I misunderstood."

content.speaker: John
content.text: "오해했던 것 같아요"? Is it better than just saying "I was wrong"?

content.speaker: Mina
content.text: 네. "내가 틀렸다" 고 하는 것보다..., "내 생각이 좀 달랐던 것 같다" 라고 말하는 게 훨씬 부드럽고 자연스럽거든요.
content.translation: Yes. It's much softer and more natural than saying "I was wrong."

content.speaker: Mina
content.text: 오늘은 꼬여버린 관계를 다시 풀어주는 화해의 리액션들을 배워볼게요!
content.translation: Today, we'll learn 'reconciliation reactions' that untangle twisted relationships!

content.speaker: John
content.text: 제가 오해했던 것 같아요.

content.speaker: John
content.text: Adding "것 같아요" at the end makes it sound like I'm being careful, right?

content.speaker: Mina
content.text: 맞아요! "오해했어요" 라고 딱 잘라 말하는 것보다 훨씬 조심스럽고 정중하게 들리죠.
content.translation: Right! It sounds much more careful and polite than saying "I misunderstood" bluntly.

insight.title: 제가 오해했던 것 같아요
insight.usage_tip: 제가(I/Polite) + 오해했던(That I misunderstood) + 것 같아요(It seems like). Using '~것 같아요' is a common way to soften an admission or an opinion in Korean.

content.speaker: John
content.text: And how should I respond when I hear the real reason?

content.speaker: Mina
content.text: 그럴 땐..., "그랬군요. 저는 그것도 모르고..." 라고 해보세요.
content.translation: In that case, try saying "I see, I didn't know that..."

content.speaker: Mina
content.text: 내가 몰랐다는 사실을 인정하면서 미안함을 전달하는 아주 좋은 리액션이에요.
content.translation: It's a very good reaction that conveys apology while admitting the fact that you didn't know.

insight.title: 저는 그것도 모르고
insight.usage_tip: This phrase implies that if you had known the situation, you wouldn't have felt that way. It's a subtle way to apologize.

content.speaker: John
content.text: Okay, let's jump in. I thought Mina was ignoring my lunch invitation, but I found out she was in a long meeting with her boss.

content.speaker: John
content.text: 미나. 아까 제가 점심 같이 먹자고 했는데 대답이 없으셔서... 사실 조금 서운했거든요.
content.translation: Mina, I asked you to have lunch earlier, but there was no reply... actually, I was a bit hurt.

content.speaker: Mina
content.text: 아! 존! 정말 죄송해요. 아침부터 계속 회의가 있어서 핸드폰을 아예 못 봤어요.
content.translation: Ah, John! I'm so sorry. I had meetings all morning, so I couldn't check my phone at all.

content.speaker: John
content.text: 그랬군요! 저는 그것도 모르고...
content.translation: Ah, I see! I didn't know that...

insight.title: 그랬군요
insight.usage_tip: Use this to show you've accepted their explanation.

content.speaker: John
content.text: 제가 혼자 오해했던 것 같아요. 바쁘신데 신경 쓰게 해서 미안해요.
content.translation: I think I misunderstood on my own. I'm sorry for making you worry when you're busy.

insight.title: 제가 혼자 오해했던 것 같아요
insight.usage_tip: Adding '혼자' (alone) emphasizes that it was your own internal mistake, making it easier for the other person to accept.

content.speaker: Mina
content.text: 아니에요. 제가 미리 말을 못 해서 미안해요. 내일 점심은 제가 살게요!
content.translation: No, I'm sorry I couldn't tell you in advance. Tomorrow's lunch is on me!

content.speaker: John
content.text: Wow, once I admitted my mistake, the tension disappeared instantly!

content.speaker: Mina
content.text: 맞아요. 오해는 빨리 풀수록 좋으니까요. 솔직한 리액션이 최고의 해결책이죠.
content.translation: Right. The sooner you clear a misunderstanding, the better. Honest reactions are the best solution.

content.speaker: John
content.text: Let's review our "clearing the air" reactions one last time.

content.speaker: John
content.text: 제가 오해했던 것 같아요!
content.translation: I think I misunderstood!

content.speaker: John
content.text: 그랬군요, 저는 그것도 모르고!
content.translation: I see, I didn't know that!

content.speaker: John
content.text: 기분 나쁘셨다면 죄송해요!
content.translation: I'm sorry if I offended you!

content.speaker: John
content.text: 이제 다 풀렸어요!
content.translation: Everything is cleared up now!

content.speaker: John
content.text: 먼저 말해줘서 고마워요!
content.translation: Thank you for telling me first!

content.speaker: Mina
content.text: 여러분도 주변 사람과 오해가 생겼다면, 주저하지 말고 먼저 따뜻한 리액션으로 다가가 보세요.
content.translation: Everyone, if a misunderstanding arises with someone around you, don't hesitate and approach them first with a warm reaction.

content.speaker: Mina
content.text: "제가 오해했던 것 같아요."
content.translation: I think I misunderstood.

content.speaker: John
content.text: That's all for today. See you in the next episode.

content.speaker: Mina
content.text: 다음에 또 만나요. 안녕~!
content.translation: See you again next time. Bye~!

vocabulary.example: 사소한 오해 때문에 친구와 싸웠어요. (I fought with my friend because of a trivial misunderstanding.)
내 실수를 깨닫고 나니 정말 민망하네요. (It's really embarrassing after realizing my mistake.)
사과를 하고 나서야 친구와의 관계를 회복했어요. (I restored the relationship with my friend only after apologizing.)

vocabulary.meaning: To conclude / To jump to a conclusion
vocabulary.example: 사실을 확인하기 전까지는 함부로 단정 짓지 마세요. (Don't jump to conclusions carelessly before confirming the facts.)
우리는 대화를 통해 서로 화해했어요. (We reconciled with each other through conversation.)
제 일 때문에 너무 신경 쓰지 마세요. (Don't worry too much about my work.)
